title: Fixing Docker Compose Container Port Mapping Validation

statement.markdown: Write a function to validate Docker Compose container port mappings. A container port mapping is valid if it does not contain any duplicate ports or services. For example, the following mapping is valid: `8080:8080` or `8080:service1,8081:service2`. However, the following mapping is invalid: `8080:8080,8080:service1` because it contains a duplicate port `8080`. Your function should take a string of container port mappings as input and return `true` if the mappings are valid and `false` otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: A string of container port mappings, where each mapping is in the format `port:service` or `port:service1,port:service2`.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean value indicating whether the container port mappings are valid or not.
statement.notes: You can assume that the input string is well-formed and does not contain any invalid characters.

constraints.description: The maximum number of container port mappings.

examples.explanation: The port mapping is valid because it does not contain any duplicate ports or services.
The port mapping is valid because it does not contain any duplicate ports or services.
The port mapping is invalid because it contains a duplicate port `8080`.

hints.text: You can split the input string into individual port mappings using the comma as a delimiter.

starterCode.javascript: function validatePortMappings(mappings) { // Implement your solution here }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def validate_port_mappings(self, mappings: str) -> bool: pass

buggyStarterCode.javascript: function validatePortMappings(mappings) { let mappingsArray = mappings.split(','); let portSet = new Set(); for (let mapping of mappingsArray) { let [port, service] = mapping.split(':'); if (portSet.has(port)) return false; portSet.add(port); } return true; }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def validate_port_mappings(self, mappings: str) -> bool: port_set = set(); for mapping in mappings.split(','): port, service = mapping.split(':'); if port in port_set: return False; port_set.add(port); return True;
